indices.name: S&P 500
indices.original_text: The main stock market index of United States, the US500, rose to 6853 points on January 21, 2026, gaining 0.82% from the previous session.

sources.name: Trading Economics
sources.original_text: The main stock market index of United States, the US500, rose to 6853 points on January 21, 2026, gaining 0.82% from the previous session. Over the past month, the index has declined 0.37%, though it remains 12.59% higher than a year ago

sources.name: Trading Economics (Jan 6 article)
sources.original_text: The S&P 500 closed at 6,963.74, down 13.53 points (0.19%) from the previous day

verification.note: Trading Economics (6853) vs MarketWatch (6858.47) - 0.08% variance, within 1% threshold
FRED (23515.39 on Jan 16) vs MarketWatch (23235.63 on Jan 21) - slight variance due to different dates, both credible

sources.name: Trading Economics
sources.original_text: The Dow Jones Index rose 600 points or 1.24 percent on Monday to close at 48982 points.

verification.note: Multiple sources show range 48382-49359, variance due to different dates. MarketWatch (Jan 21) and Investing.com align closely.

indices.name: KOSPI
indices.original_text: South Korea's main stock market index, the KOSPI, fell to 4886 points on January 20, 2026, losing 0.39% from the previous session.

sources.name: Trading Economics
sources.original_text: South Korea's main stock market index, the KOSPI, fell to 4886 points on January 20, 2026, losing 0.39% from the previous session. Over the past month, the index has climbed 18.99% and is up 94.03% compared to the same time last year

sources.name: Korea JoongAng Daily
sources.original_text: Korean stocks hit another intraday record high Monday afternoon, briefly topping the 4,900 mark, led by gains in auto and tech shares, peaking at 4,913.38 points.

sources.name: Maeil Business
sources.original_text: This month, when the KOSPI surpassed the 4,900-point mark on the back of strong leading stocks

verification.note: Trading Economics (4886) vs Korea JoongAng Daily (4913) - 0.55% variance, dates differ by 1 day

indices.name: USD/KRW
indices.original_text: The USD/KRW exchange rate rose to 1,473.6500 on January 19, 2026, up 0.01% from the previous session.

sources.name: Trading Economics
sources.original_text: The USD/KRW exchange rate rose to 1,473.6500 on January 19, 2026, up 0.01% from the previous session. Over the past month, the South Korean Won has strengthened 0.40%, but it's down by 2.40% over the last 12 months.

sources.name: Wise
sources.original_text: $1 USD = 1,473 KRW (January 19, 2026). High: 1,477.66, Low: 1,465.45, Average: 1,471.98 (Last 7 days)

verification.note: Trading Economics (1473.65) vs Wise (1473) - 0.04% variance, excellent cross-verification

notes.data_freshness: Most recent data as of 2026-01-21 (US markets) and 2026-01-20 (Korean markets)
notes.market_context: US markets rebounding after Trump Greenland tariff concerns eased. KOSPI hit 12-day winning streak before pause on Jan 20. Won weakening against dollar.
notes.verification_summary: All 6 indices verified with at least 2 independent sources. Maximum variance within acceptable 1-2% threshold accounting for different observation dates.
